## Callbacks

All callbacks are optional. Define any in `main.lua` and the engine calls them automatically.

```lua
function lurek.load()  -- Called once after the script is loaded.
function lurek.update( dt : number )  -- Called every frame; dt = elapsed seconds.
function lurek.draw()  -- Called every frame for rendering.
function lurek.keypressed( key : string, scancode : string, isrepeat : boolean )  -- Key press event.
function lurek.keyreleased( key : string, scancode : string )  -- Key release event.
function lurek.textinput( text : string )  -- Unicode character typed.
function lurek.mousepressed( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button press.
function lurek.mousereleased( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button release.
function lurek.wheelmoved( x : number, y : number )  -- Mouse wheel scroll.
function lurek.gamepadpressed( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button press.
function lurek.gamepadreleased( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button release.
function lurek.gamepadaxis( id : number, axis : string, value : number )  -- Gamepad axis; value in -1..1.
function lurek.joystickadded( id : number )  -- Gamepad connected.
function lurek.joystickremoved( id : number )  -- Gamepad disconnected.
function lurek.touchpressed( id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch begin.
function lurek.touchmoved(  id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch move.
function lurek.touchreleased(id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch end.
function lurek.focus( focused : boolean )  -- Window focus change.
function lurek.visible( visible : boolean )  -- Window show/hide.
function lurek.resize( w : number, h : number )  -- Window resized.
function lurek.quit()  -- Return true to cancel quit.
function lurek.errorhandler( msg : string )  -- Unhandled Lua error.
```

```lua
lurek.input.setTextInput( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables Unicode text input mode
```

### `Cursor`

Lua-side wrapper around a mouse cursor handle.

```lua
Cursor:getType() -> string  -- Returns the cursor type as "system" or "custom"
Cursor:release() -> nil  -- Releases the cursor resource (no-op on desktop)
```

## `lurek.time` {#timer}

> `luna.time` - Frame timing, FPS tracking, and scheduled Lua callbacks.

*Coverage: 27/27 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.time.getAverageDelta() -> number  -- Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds
lurek.time.getDelta() -> number  -- Returns the delta time in seconds for the current frame
lurek.time.getFPS() -> number  -- Returns the current frames-per-second measurement
lurek.time.getMicroTime() -> number  -- Returns the high-resolution elapsed time since engine start in seconds
lurek.time.getPhysicsDelta() -> number  -- Returns the fixed timestep used by `process_physics` callbacks (seconds)
lurek.time.getTime() -> number  -- Returns the total elapsed time since engine start in seconds
lurek.time.newScheduler() -> Scheduler  -- Creates a new independent Scheduler for managing timed callbacks
lurek.time.setPhysicsDelta( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the fixed timestep for `process_physics` callbacks (seconds)
lurek.time.sleep( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Suspends execution for the given number of seconds
lurek.time.step() -> number  -- Advances the timer by one frame, returning the delta time
```

### `Scheduler`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Scheduler`] with per-event callback storage.

```lua
Scheduler:after( delay : number, func : function ) -> integer  -- Schedules a callback to fire once after a delay
Scheduler:cancel( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a scheduled event by its numeric ID
Scheduler:cancelAll() -> integer  -- Cancels all scheduled events and returns the count removed
Scheduler:cancelNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a scheduled event by its string name
Scheduler:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of active scheduled events
Scheduler:getInterval( id : integer ) -> number?  -- Returns the base interval in seconds for an event, or nil
Scheduler:getRemaining( id : integer ) -> number?  -- Returns the seconds remaining until the next fire for an event, or nil
Scheduler:getRepeatCount( id : integer ) -> integer?  -- Returns the repeat count remaining for an event, or nil
Scheduler:getTimeScale() -> number  -- Returns the current time-scale multiplier
Scheduler:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the scheduler has no active events
Scheduler:isPaused( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given event is currently paused
Scheduler:pause( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Pauses a scheduled event by its ID
Scheduler:resetEvent( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Resets an event's remaining time back to its original interval
Scheduler:resume( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Resumes a paused event by its ID
Scheduler:setInterval( id : integer, interval : number ) -> boolean  -- Changes the repeat interval of an existing event
Scheduler:setTimeScale( scale : number ) -> nil  -- Sets a global time-scale multiplier for this scheduler
Scheduler:update( dt : number ) -> integer  -- Advances all timers by dt seconds, firing due callbacks
```

## `lurek.math` {#math}

> `lurek.math` — Math utilities: random generators, transforms, Bezier curves, tweening,
> spatial hashing, noise, easing, polygon triangulation, and color-space conversion.

*Coverage: 132/132 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.math.abs( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the absolute value of x
lurek.math.acos( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the arccosine of x, in radians
lurek.math.angleBetween( x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number ) -> number  -- Returns the angle in radians from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2)
lurek.math.applyEasing( name : string, t : number ) -> number  -- Applies a named easing function to progress value t
lurek.math.asin( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the arcsine of x, in radians
lurek.math.atan( y : number, x : number? ) -> number  -- Returns the arctangent of x (or atan2(y, x) when two args given)
lurek.math.atan2( y : number, x : number ) -> number  -- Returns atan(y/x) using the signs of both args to determine the quadrant
lurek.math.bresenham( x1 : integer, y1 : integer, x2 : integer, y2 : integer ) -> table  -- Rasterizes a line from (x1,y1) to (x2,y2) using Bresenham's algorithm. Returns a table of {x,y} tables
lurek.math.ceil( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the smallest integer ≥ x
lurek.math.circleContainsPoint( cx : number, cy : number, r : number, px : number, py : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the point (px, py) lies inside the circle
lurek.math.circleIntersectsCircle( x1 : number, y1 : number, r1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number, r2 : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if two circles overlap
lurek.math.circleIntersectsLine( cx : number, cy : number, r : number, lx1 : number, ly1 : number, lx2 : number, ly2 : number ) -> boolean  -- Tests an infinite line against a circle. Returns hit, then two optional hit-point pairs
lurek.math.circleIntersectsSegment( cx : number, cy : number, r : number, sx1 : number, sy1 : number, sx2 : number, sy2 : number ) -> boolean  -- Tests a line segment against a circle. Returns hit, then two optional hit-point pairs
lurek.math.clamp( x : number, lo : number, hi : number ) -> number  -- Returns x clamped to [lo, hi]
lurek.math.closestPointOnSegment( px : number, py : number, x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number ) -> number  -- Returns the closest point on segment (x1,y1)-(x2,y2) to point (px,py)
lurek.math.convexHull( points : table ) -> table  -- Computes the convex hull of a flat {x1,y1,...} point list. Returns a flat table
lurek.math.cos( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the cosine of x (radians)
lurek.math.deg( rad : number ) -> number  -- Converts radians to degrees
lurek.math.delaunayTriangulate( points : table ) -> table  -- Delaunay triangulation of a flat {x1,y1,...} point list. Returns a table of flat 6-number triangle tables
lurek.math.distance( x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number ) -> number  -- Returns the Euclidean distance between (x1,y1) and (x2,y2)
lurek.math.distanceSq( x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number ) -> number  -- Returns the squared Euclidean distance between (x1,y1) and (x2,y2) (avoids sqrt)
lurek.math.exp( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns e raised to the power x
lurek.math.fbm( x : number, y : number, seed : integer?, octaves : integer?, lacunarity : number?, gain : number? ) -> number  -- Returns fractal Brownian motion noise at (x, y)
lurek.math.floor( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the largest integer ≤ x
lurek.math.fmod( x : number, y : number ) -> number  -- Returns the remainder of x / y (fmod)
lurek.math.gammaToLinear( c : number ) -> number  -- Converts a gamma-encoded sRGB value to linear space
lurek.math.inBack( t : number ) -> number  -- Back ease-in — overshoots slightly before settling at the target
lurek.math.inBounce( t : number ) -> number  -- Bounce ease-in
lurek.math.inCubic( t : number ) -> number  -- Cubic ease-in — acceleration starts slowly then increases sharply
lurek.math.inElastic( t : number ) -> number  -- Elastic ease-in
lurek.math.inExpo( t : number ) -> number  -- Exponential ease-in
lurek.math.inOutCubic( t : number ) -> number  -- Cubic ease-in-out
lurek.math.inOutExpo( t : number ) -> number  -- Exponential ease-in-out
lurek.math.inOutQuad( t : number ) -> number  -- Quadratic ease-in-out
lurek.math.inOutQuart( t : number ) -> number  -- Quartic ease-in-out
lurek.math.inOutSine( t : number ) -> number  -- Sinusoidal ease-in-out
lurek.math.inQuad( t : number ) -> number  -- Quadratic ease-in
lurek.math.inQuart( t : number ) -> number  -- Quartic ease-in
lurek.math.inSine( t : number ) -> number  -- Sinusoidal ease-in
lurek.math.isConvex( polygon : table ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the polygon (flat table {x1,y1,...}) is convex
lurek.math.lerp( a : number, b : number, t : number ) -> number  -- Linear interpolation between a and b by fraction t
lurek.math.lineIntersect( x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number, x3 : number, y3 : number, x4 : number, y4 : number ) -> number?  -- Infinite line intersection. Returns (x, y) or (nil, nil) if lines are parallel
lurek.math.linear( t : number ) -> number  -- Linear easing (identity)
lurek.math.linearToGamma( c : number ) -> number  -- Converts a linear-space value to gamma-encoded sRGB
lurek.math.log( x : number, b : number? ) -> number  -- Returns the natural log of x, or log base b if b is supplied
lurek.math.max() -> number  -- Returns the largest of the supplied numbers
lurek.math.min() -> number  -- Returns the smallest of the supplied numbers
lurek.math.newBezierCurve( points : table ) -> BezierCurve  -- Creates a new BezierCurve from a flat table of coordinates {x1,y1, x2,y2, ...}
lurek.math.newNoiseGenerator( seed : integer? ) -> NoiseGenerator  -- Creates a new seeded noise generator
lurek.math.newRandomGenerator( seed : integer? ) -> RandomGenerator  -- Creates a new random number generator with an optional seed
lurek.math.newSpatialHash( cellSize : number ) -> SpatialHash  -- Creates a new SpatialHash with the given cell size
lurek.math.newTransform( x : number?, y : number?, angle : number?, sx : number?, sy : number?, ox : number?, oy : number?, kx : number?, ky : number? ) -> Transform  -- Creates a new Transform, optionally initialised from full parameters
lurek.math.newTween( duration : number, easingName : string? ) -> Tween  -- Creates a new Tween with the given duration and easing name
lurek.math.outBack( t : number ) -> number  -- Back ease-out — overshoots the target then snaps back into place
lurek.math.outBounce( t : number ) -> number  -- Bounce ease-out
lurek.math.outCubic( t : number ) -> number  -- Cubic ease-out
lurek.math.outElastic( t : number ) -> number  -- Elastic ease-out
lurek.math.outExpo( t : number ) -> number  -- Exponential ease-out
lurek.math.outQuad( t : number ) -> number  -- Quadratic ease-out
lurek.math.outQuart( t : number ) -> number  -- Quartic ease-out
lurek.math.outSine( t : number ) -> number  -- Sinusoidal ease-out
lurek.math.perlin2d( x : number, y : number, seed : integer? ) -> number  -- Returns 2D Perlin noise at (x, y) with the given seed
lurek.math.perlin3d( x : number, y : number, z : number, seed : integer? ) -> number  -- Returns 3D Perlin noise at (x, y, z) with the given seed
lurek.math.pointInPolygon( polygon : table, px : number, py : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if (px, py) is inside the polygon given as a flat {x1,y1,...} table
lurek.math.polygonArea( polygon : table ) -> number  -- Returns the signed area of a polygon given as a flat {x1,y1,...} table
lurek.math.polygonCentroid( polygon : table ) -> number  -- Returns the centroid (cx, cy) of a polygon given as a flat {x1,y1,...} table
lurek.math.pow( x : number, y : number ) -> number  -- Returns x raised to the power y
lurek.math.rad( deg : number ) -> number  -- Converts degrees to radians
lurek.math.random( min_or_max : number?, max : number? ) -> number  -- Returns a pseudo-random number in [0,1) with no args,
lurek.math.randomInt( lo : integer, hi : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns a pseudo-random integer in [lo, hi] (inclusive)
```

## `lurek.system` {#system}

> System Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Lurek2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for system api-related operations and data management.
> Key types exported from this module: `PowerState`.
> Primary functions: `get_processor_count()`, `get_memory_size()`, `open_url()`, `get_preferred_locales()`.

*Coverage: 22/22 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.system.getArch()  -- Returns the CPU architecture string for the current machine
lurek.system.getArgs() -> table  -- Returns the command-line arguments as a table
lurek.system.getBatchResults( results : table )  -- Returns the output table from the most recently completed runBatch call
lurek.system.getClipboardText()  -- Returns the current contents of the system clipboard
lurek.system.getDebugOverlay()  -- Returns whether the debug overlay is currently visible
lurek.system.getEnv( name : string )  -- Returns the value of the named OS environment variable, or nil if not set
lurek.system.getInfo()  -- Returns a table of system information including OS name, CPU model, and installed RAM
lurek.system.getLastError()  -- Returns the last unhandled error message, or nil
lurek.system.getLogLevel()  -- Returns the name of the current minimum log level for runtime messages
lurek.system.getMemorySize()  -- Returns the total amount of installed system RAM in megabytes
lurek.system.getOS()  -- Returns the host operating system name ('Windows', 'Linux', 'macOS')
lurek.system.getPowerInfo()  -- Returns battery state, percentage charged, and estimated time remaining
lurek.system.getPreferredLocales()  -- Returns an ordered list of the user's preferred locale strings (e.g. 'en-US')
lurek.system.getProcessorCount()  -- Returns the number of logical CPU cores available
lurek.system.getVersion()  -- Returns the Lurek2D engine version string
lurek.system.log( level : string, message : string )  -- Emit a log message from Lua at the specified level
lurek.system.openURL( url : string )  -- Opens a URL in the system's default browser
lurek.system.parseArgs( args : table? )  -- Parses a command-line argument string and returns a structured key/value table
lurek.system.runBatch( tasks : table, opts : table? )  -- Runs a list of shell commands in parallel and returns immediately without blocking
lurek.system.setClipboardText( text : string )  -- Replaces the system clipboard contents with the given string
lurek.system.setDebugOverlay( enabled : boolean )  -- Shows or hides the FPS/draw-call debug overlay
lurek.system.setLogLevel( level : string )  -- Sets the minimum severity level for runtime log messages
```

```lua
lurek.tilemap.hexDistance( q1 : integer, r1 : integer, q2 : integer, r2 : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the hex distance between two axial coordinates
lurek.tilemap.hexLine( q1 : integer, r1 : integer, q2 : integer, r2 : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all hex cells along a line between two axial coordinates as a table
lurek.tilemap.hexNeighbors( q : integer, r : integer ) -> table  -- Returns the six axial neighbor coordinates as a table of {q, r} pairs
lurek.tilemap.hexReflect( q : integer, r : integer, centerQ : integer, centerR : integer, axis : string ) -> integer  -- Reflects hex coordinates across an axis through the center
lurek.tilemap.hexRing( q : integer, r : integer, radius : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all cells at exactly radius distance from (q, r) as a table
lurek.tilemap.hexRotate( q : integer, r : integer, centerQ : integer, centerR : integer, steps : integer ) -> integer  -- Rotates hex coordinates around a center by steps x 60 degrees clockwise
lurek.tilemap.hexRound( q : number, r : number ) -> integer  -- Rounds fractional axial coordinates to the nearest hex cell
lurek.tilemap.hexSpiral( q : integer, r : integer, radius : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all hex cells from center outward to radius, ring by ring, as a table
lurek.tilemap.isoDirectionFromAngle( angle : number ) -> integer  -- Snaps an angle (in radians) to the nearest isometric direction (1-4)
lurek.tilemap.isoDirectionName( direction : integer ) -> string  -- Returns the name of an isometric direction (1-4)
lurek.tilemap.isoRotate( direction : integer, steps : integer ) -> integer  -- Rotates an isometric direction (1-4) clockwise by steps
lurek.tilemap.loadTMX( xml : string ) -> table  -- Parses a TMX XML string and returns a table with map metadata and layers
lurek.tilemap.newAutoTileSheet( tileWidth : integer, tileHeight : integer, layout : string? ) -> AutoTileSheet  -- Creates a new AutoTileSheet with the given tile dimensions and layout
lurek.tilemap.newChunkMap( chunkSize : integer? ) -> ChunkMap  -- Creates a new ChunkMap with the given chunk size
lurek.tilemap.newIsoMap( width : integer, height : integer, tileW : integer, tileH : integer, levelHeight : integer ) -> IsoMap  -- Creates a new IsoMap with no levels
lurek.tilemap.newMapBlock( width : integer, height : integer, layers : integer?, segmentSize : integer? ) -> MapBlock  -- Creates a new MapBlock with the given dimensions
lurek.tilemap.newMapGen( group : MapGroup, preset : string, segmentSize : integer, segmentSize : integer? ) -> MapGen  -- Creates a MapGen from a MapGroup, a preset name or dimensions, and a segment size
lurek.tilemap.newMapGroup( name : string ) -> MapGroup  -- Creates a new empty MapGroup with the given name
lurek.tilemap.newMapScript() -> MapScript  -- Creates a new empty MapScript procedural generation script
lurek.tilemap.newTileMap( tileWidth : integer, tileHeight : integer, chunkSize : integer? ) -> TileMap  -- Creates a new TileMap with the given tile size and chunk size
lurek.tilemap.newTileSet( firstGid : integer, tileCount : integer, columns : integer, tileWidth : integer, tileHeight : integer, spacing : integer?, margin : integer? ) -> TileSet  -- Creates a new TileSet with the given atlas layout parameters
lurek.tilemap.toScreenHex( q : integer, r : integer, size : number ) -> number  -- Converts axial hex coordinates to screen position (pointy-top layout)
lurek.tilemap.toScreenIso( tx : number, ty : number, tileW : number, tileH : number ) -> number  -- Converts tile coordinates to screen position using diamond isometric projection
```

```lua
lurek.entity.newUniverse() -> Universe  -- Creates a new empty ECS universe
```

### `Universe`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Universe`] ECS world.

```lua
Universe:addSystem( system : table ) -> nil  -- Adds a system table to the universe
Universe:addTag( id : integer, tag : string ) -> nil  -- Attaches a string tag to an entity
Universe:bitmapTag( id : integer, name : string ) -> nil  -- Adds a bitmap tag to an entity
Universe:bitmapUntag( id : integer, name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a bitmap tag from an entity
Universe:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all entities, components, tags, layers, and systems. Blueprints are preserved
Universe:defineTag( name : string ) -> integer  -- Defines a bitmap tag name, returning its bit index
Universe:each( name : string, callback : function ) -> nil  -- Calls callback(id, value) for every entity with the named component
Universe:emit( event : string ) -> nil  -- Emits a named event to all systems that implement the handler
Universe:get( id : integer, name : string ) -> table  -- Returns the component value for an entity, or nil if missing
Universe:getBitmapTagBit( name : string ) -> integer?  -- Returns the bit index for a bitmap tag name, or nil if undefined
Universe:getBlueprintComponents( name : string ) -> table  -- Returns a deep copy of a blueprint's component table, or nil
Universe:getChildren( parent_id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all direct child entity IDs
Universe:getComponents( id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all component names for an entity
Universe:getEntities() -> table  -- Returns all alive entity IDs
Universe:getEntitiesByLayer( layer : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities on a specific layer
Universe:getEntitiesByTag( tag : string ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with the given string tag
Universe:getEntitiesSorted() -> table  -- Returns all alive entities sorted by layer then ID
Universe:getEntityCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of alive entities
Universe:getLayer( id : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the layer for an entity, defaulting to zero
Universe:getParent( child_id : integer ) -> integer?  -- Returns the parent entity ID, or nil if unparented
Universe:getSystemCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of registered systems
Universe:getTags( id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all string tags for an entity
Universe:has( id : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity has the named component
Universe:hasBitmapTag( id : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity has the given bitmap tag
Universe:hasBlueprint( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if a blueprint with the given name exists
Universe:hasTag( id : integer, tag : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity carries the given tag
Universe:isAlive( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity ID is currently alive
Universe:kill( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Destroys the entity with the given ID, freeing its slot for reuse
Universe:killRecursive( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Kills an entity and all its descendants recursively
Universe:listBlueprints() -> table  -- Returns all defined blueprint names
Universe:query( names : string ) -> table  -- Returns entity IDs that have all listed component names
Universe:queryBitmapAll( names : table ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with all of the listed bitmap tags
Universe:queryBitmapAny( names : table ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with any of the listed bitmap tags
Universe:queryBitmapTag( name : string ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with the given bitmap tag
Universe:release() -> nil  -- Releases all universe state, equivalent to clear
Universe:remove( id : integer, name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a component from an entity
Universe:removeBlueprint( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a blueprint definition
Universe:removeSystem( system : table ) -> nil  -- Removes a system table from the universe
Universe:removeTag( id : integer, tag : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a string tag from an entity
Universe:render() -> nil  -- Calls render(system, world) on each registered system
Universe:set( id : integer, name : string, value : table ) -> nil  -- Sets a component value on an entity
Universe:setLayer( id : integer, layer : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the layer for an entity
Universe:spawn() -> integer  -- Creates a new entity and returns its packed ID
Universe:update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls update(system, world, dt) on each registered system
```

## `lurek.scene` {#scene}

> `lurek.scene` — Scene stack management, transitions, registry, and depth-sorted rendering.

*Coverage: 33/33 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.scene.clear() -> nil  -- Clears all scenes from the stack, calling leave on each
lurek.scene.draw() -> nil  -- Draws all scenes in the stack from bottom to top (legacy name; prefer `render`)
lurek.scene.getCurrent() -> table?  -- Returns the current top scene table, or nil if the stack is empty
lurek.scene.getData( key : string ) -> table?  -- Returns a value from the inter-scene data store, or nil if not found
lurek.scene.getRegistered( name : string ) -> table?  -- Returns a registered scene table by name, or nil if not found
lurek.scene.getRegisteredNames() -> table  -- Returns a list of all registered scene names
lurek.scene.getStackSize() -> integer  -- Returns the number of scenes on the stack
lurek.scene.getTransitionProgress() -> number  -- Returns the transition progress from 0.0 to 1.0
lurek.scene.hasData( key : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the given key exists in the data store
lurek.scene.hasRegistered( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if a scene is registered under the given name
lurek.scene.isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the scene stack is empty
lurek.scene.isTransitioning() -> boolean  -- Returns true if a scene transition is currently active
lurek.scene.newDepthSorter() -> DepthSorter  -- Creates a new DepthSorter for z-ordered draw batching
lurek.scene.pop( transition : string?, duration : number? ) -> nil  -- Pops the top scene from the stack with an optional transition
lurek.scene.popTo( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Pops scenes until the named scene is on top, calling leave on each removed
lurek.scene.process( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls `scene:ready(self)` on the top scene if not yet fired, then `scene:process(dt)`
lurek.scene.processLate( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls `scene:process_late(dt)` on the topmost scene (after process, before render)
lurek.scene.processPhysics( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls `scene:process_physics(dt)` on the topmost scene (fixed timestep)
lurek.scene.push( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, params : table? ) -> nil  -- Pushes a scene table onto the stack with an optional transition
lurek.scene.registerScene( name : string, scene : table ) -> nil  -- Registers a scene table by name for later retrieval
lurek.scene.removeData( key : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a value from the inter-scene data store by key
lurek.scene.render() -> nil  -- Draws all scenes in the stack from bottom to top
lurek.scene.renderUi() -> nil  -- Draws UI overlay for all scenes in the stack from bottom to top
lurek.scene.setData( key : string, value : table ) -> nil  -- Stores a value in the inter-scene data store under the given key
lurek.scene.switchTo( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, params : table? ) -> nil  -- Replaces the top scene with a new one, calling leave and enter callbacks
lurek.scene.unregisterScene( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a scene from the registry by name
lurek.scene.update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Updates the top scene and any active transition (legacy name; prefer `process`)
```

### `DepthSorter`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`DepthSorter`] with registry-stored callbacks.

```lua
DepthSorter:add( callback : function, depth : number ) -> nil  -- Registers a draw callback at the given depth layer
DepthSorter:addObject( obj : table ) -> nil  -- Registers a table object with a draw method at the given depth
DepthSorter:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all registered callbacks without calling them
DepthSorter:flush() -> nil  -- Calls all draw callbacks in sorted depth order, then clears
DepthSorter:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of registered draw entries
DepthSorter:sort() -> nil  -- Sorts all registered callbacks by depth ascending
```

## `lurek.savegame` {#savegame}

> `luna.savegame` - Slot-based save/load system with collectors, schema versioning, and auto-save.

*Coverage: 19/19 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.savegame.newSaveManager() -> SaveManager  -- Creates a new SaveManager for slot-based save/load operations
```

### `SaveManager`

Lua-side wrapper around [`SaveManager`] with per-module callback storage.

```lua
SaveManager:collect() -> table  -- Collects data from all registered collectors into a table with metadata
SaveManager:delete( slot : string ) -> nil  -- Deletes a save file for the given slot
SaveManager:disableAutoSave() -> nil  -- Disables auto-save
SaveManager:exists( slot : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether a save file exists for the given slot
SaveManager:getSchemaVersion() -> integer  -- Returns the current schema version
SaveManager:getSlotInfo( slot : string ) -> table?  -- Returns metadata for a single slot, or nil if not found
SaveManager:getSlots() -> table  -- Returns a list of all save slots with metadata
SaveManager:getSummary() -> string  -- Returns the current summary string
SaveManager:isDirty() -> boolean  -- Returns whether data has been modified since the last save or load
SaveManager:load( slot : string ) -> boolean  -- Loads data from a slot file, applies migrations, and restores
SaveManager:markDirty() -> nil  -- Marks data as modified since the last save or load
SaveManager:reset() -> nil  -- Resets all state, removing callbacks and clearing the manager
SaveManager:restore( data : table ) -> nil  -- Restores data from a table, applying migrations and calling restorers
SaveManager:save( slot : string ) -> nil  -- Collects data and writes it to a slot file
SaveManager:setSchemaVersion( version : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the current schema version for new saves
SaveManager:setSummary( summary : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the summary string included in save metadata
SaveManager:unregister( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a named module and its callbacks
SaveManager:update( dt : number ) -> string?  -- Advances the auto-save timer, returning the slot name if a save should trigger
```

```lua
ModManager:hasMod( mod_id : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether a mod with the given ID is registered
ModManager:markForReload( mod_id : string ) -> boolean  -- Marks a registered mod for hot-reload
ModManager:registerMod( mod_ud : Mod ) -> nil  -- Registers a mod from its Mod userdata
ModManager:scanFolder( path : string ) -> table  -- Scans a directory for mods with mod.toml and registers them
ModManager:setLoadOrder( order : table ) -> nil  -- Sets an explicit load order from an array of mod ID strings
ModManager:unregisterMod( mod_id : string ) -> boolean  -- Removes a mod by ID and returns whether it was found
ModManager:validateDependencies() -> table  -- Returns an array of mod IDs with missing dependencies
```

## `lurek.localization` {#localization}

> Registers the `lurek.localization.*` internationalization and localization API.
>
> Thin Lua bridge that delegates to the [`localization`][crate::localization] domain module.

> in [`crate::localization`]; this file converts between Lua values and domain types.

*Coverage: 27/27 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.localization.buildIndex() -> table  -- Builds an inverted word index for the active locale. Returns index as {word → {keys}}
lurek.localization.categories() -> table  -- Returns unique first-path-segment category prefixes for all active locale keys
lurek.localization.getAvailableLanguages() -> table  -- Returns all loaded locale codes (alias for getLanguages)
lurek.localization.getBase() -> string  -- Returns the base/fallback language
lurek.localization.getFallbacks() -> table  -- Returns the current fallback locale array
lurek.localization.getKeys() -> table  -- Returns all known keys for the active locale
lurek.localization.getLanguage() -> string?  -- Returns the currently active locale code, or nil if unset
lurek.localization.getLanguages() -> table  -- Returns all loaded locale codes
lurek.localization.hasKey( key : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether a key exists in the active locale
lurek.localization.hasLanguage( locale : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether a locale has been loaded
lurek.localization.interpolate( template : string, vars : table ) -> string  -- Interpolates {name} placeholders in a template string
lurek.localization.keyCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of keys loaded in the active locale
lurek.localization.keysInCategory( category : string ) -> table  -- Returns all keys in the active locale whose first path segment matches category
lurek.localization.loadTable( locale : string, table : table )  -- Loads a language table under the given locale code
lurek.localization.mergeLocale( locale : string, entries : table )  -- Merges a flat key→value table into an existing locale without replacing the whole table
lurek.localization.offChange()  -- Unregisters all onChange callbacks
lurek.localization.onChange( cb : function )  -- Registers a callback invoked when setLanguage() is called (alias: onChange)
lurek.localization.onLanguageChange( cb : function )  -- Registers a callback invoked when setLanguage() is called
lurek.localization.pluralFor( n : number ) -> string  -- Returns the CLDR plural category for a number ("one" or "other", etc.)
lurek.localization.search( query : string, limit : integer? ) -> table  -- Searches active locale values for a substring query (case-insensitive). Returns {key, value} pairs
lurek.localization.searchIndexed( index : table, query : string, limit : integer? ) -> table  -- Searches the provided pre-built index for entries matching all words in query
lurek.localization.setBase( locale : string )  -- Sets the base/fallback language (adds it as first fallback)
lurek.localization.setFallbacks( locales : table )  -- Sets the ordered list of fallback locale codes tried when a key is missing
lurek.localization.setKey( locale : string, key : string, value : string )  -- Inserts or overwrites a single key in the given locale
lurek.localization.setLanguage( locale : string )  -- Sets the active translation language
lurek.localization.t( key : string, vars : table?, count : number? ) -> string  -- Translates a key against the active locale with optional variable
lurek.localization.unloadTable( locale : string ) -> boolean  -- Unloads a locale from the catalog
```

## `lurek.log` {#log}

> Structured game-level logging API (`lurek.log.*`).
>
> Exposes convenience logging functions so Lua scripts can emit messages at
> specific severity levels and query or change the active log level at
> runtime. Also supports configurable output sinks (file, memory ring buffer)
> in addition to the default stderr channel, similar to Python's `logging`
> module handlers.

*Coverage: 13/13 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.log.addSink( config : table ) -> integer  -- Registers a new output sink. Returns its numeric id
lurek.log.clearSinks()  -- Removes all registered sinks (the default stderr channel is unaffected)
lurek.log.debug( message : string, tag : string? )  -- Emits a debug-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
lurek.log.error( message : string, tag : string? )  -- Emits an error-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
lurek.log.flushFile( id : integer )  -- Flushes the OS write buffer for a file sink
lurek.log.getLevel() -> string  -- Returns the name of the currently active minimum log level
lurek.log.info( message : string, tag : string? )  -- Emits an info-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
lurek.log.listSinks() -> table  -- Returns a table describing all registered sinks
lurek.log.print( level : string, message : string, tag : string? )  -- Emits a log message at the specified level. Also dispatches to sinks
lurek.log.readMemory( id : integer, drain : boolean? ) -> table?  -- Reads entries from a memory sink. If drain=true the buffer is cleared
lurek.log.removeSink( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes a sink by id. Returns true if one was removed
lurek.log.setLevel( level : string )  -- Sets the minimum severity level for the default log channel
lurek.log.warn( message : string, tag : string? )  -- Emits a warn-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
```

---

## `lurek.debugbridge` {#debugbridge}

> Registers the `lurek.debugbridge.*` TCP debug server API.
>
> Embeds a JSON-over-TCP server (127.0.0.1 only) inside the running game.
> External tools (VS Code extension, MCP server) connect to inspect and
> control the game at runtime. Network I/O runs on a background thread;
> Lua-dependent methods are queued and dispatched via `poll()` on the main thread.

*Coverage: 14/14 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.debugbridge.broadcast( event : string, json_data : string )  -- Broadcasts a JSON event to all connected clients
lurek.debugbridge.capturePrint( msg : string, source : string?, line : integer? )  -- Captures a print message and broadcasts it to connected clients
lurek.debugbridge.clearPrintHistory()  -- Clears the print history
lurek.debugbridge.getClientCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of connected TCP clients
lurek.debugbridge.getPerformance() -> table  -- Returns performance statistics
lurek.debugbridge.getPort() -> integer  -- Returns the server port (0 if not running)
lurek.debugbridge.getPrintHistory( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns the print history
lurek.debugbridge.isRunning() -> bool  -- Returns whether the server is currently running
lurek.debugbridge.isScreenshotRequested() -> bool  -- Returns whether a screenshot is currently requested
lurek.debugbridge.poll()  -- Poll for pending Lua-dependent requests from TCP clients
lurek.debugbridge.requestScreenshot( scale : integer? )  -- Flags a screenshot request for the next frame
lurek.debugbridge.setMaxPrintHistory( max : integer )  -- Sets the maximum print history size
lurek.debugbridge.start( port : u16? ) -> boolean  -- Start the TCP debug server on 127.0.0.1:port
lurek.debugbridge.stop()  -- Stop the TCP debug server and close all connections
```

---

```lua
lurek.docs.checkStaleness( catalog_ud : userdata, source_dir : string ) -> table  -- Compare catalog entries against source files in a directory for staleness
lurek.docs.coverage( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Return (documented_count, total_live_count) coverage tuple
lurek.docs.coverageModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Return (documented_count, total_live_count) for a single module
lurek.docs.describe( qualified_name : string, description : string )  -- Inject or update a description for a named API entry
lurek.docs.exportAll( catalog_ud : userdata, output_dir : string )  -- Export completions.json, hover.json, and signatures.json to a directory
lurek.docs.exportCheatsheet( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export a one-line-per-function plain-text cheatsheet
lurek.docs.exportCompletions( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export VS Code IntelliSense completions JSON to a file
lurek.docs.exportHover( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export VS Code hover JSON to a file
lurek.docs.exportMarkdown( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export a Markdown API reference file
lurek.docs.exportSignatures( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export VS Code signature-help JSON to a file
lurek.docs.getCatalog()  -- Return the current internal catalog as an ApiCatalog userdata
lurek.docs.loadAll( directory : string )  -- Load all .toml files in a directory and merge into a single ApiCatalog
lurek.docs.loadToml( path : string )  -- Load a TOML doc file into an ApiCatalog
lurek.docs.quality( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Calculate quality metrics for a catalog or the internal catalog
lurek.docs.qualityModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Calculate quality metrics for a single module
lurek.docs.reflectLive( ns : string? ) -> table  -- Walks the live lurek.* Lua table and returns a structured reflection of all
lurek.docs.reflectTable( tbl : table, name : string? ) -> table  -- Reflects any Lua table, returning a structure describing its keys,
lurek.docs.resetCatalog()  -- Clear all entries from the internal catalog
lurek.docs.scan( opts : table? )  -- Scan the lurek.* namespace to build an API catalog from live bindings
lurek.docs.scanModule( module_name : string )  -- Scan a single module's bindings
lurek.docs.schema( rules : table, name : string? ) -> userdata  -- Creates a Schema validator from a rules table
lurek.docs.setParamInfo( qualified_name : string, params : table )  -- Set the parameter metadata for a catalog entry
lurek.docs.setReturnInfo( qualified_name : string, returns : table )  -- Set the return type metadata for a catalog entry
lurek.docs.validate( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Validate catalog completeness against the live lurek.* bindings
lurek.docs.validateModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Validate a single module against the live lurek.<module>.* bindings
```

### `ApiCatalog`

Wraps a catalog snapshot of API entries for Lua access.

```lua
ApiCatalog:entryCount( module : string? ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of entries, optionally scoped to a module
ApiCatalog:filter( predicate : function )  -- Returns a new catalog containing only entries for which predicate returns true
ApiCatalog:getEntries( module : string? ) -> table  -- Returns all entries, optionally filtered to a single module
ApiCatalog:getEntry( qualified_name : string )  -- Returns a single entry by qualified name, or nil
ApiCatalog:getModules() -> table  -- Returns a sorted list of module names present in the catalog
ApiCatalog:getTypeMethods( qualified_name : string ) -> table  -- Returns entries that are methods of the given type qualified name
ApiCatalog:getTypes( module_name : string ) -> table  -- Returns the names of all entries with kind "type" in the given module
ApiCatalog:merge( other : userdata )  -- Returns a new catalog that is the union of this and another catalog, with other overriding duplicates
ApiCatalog:search( query : string ) -> table  -- Returns a table of entries whose name, qualified name, or description contains query
ApiCatalog:toJSON() -> string  -- Serialises the catalog to a pretty-printed JSON string
ApiCatalog:toTable() -> table  -- Converts the catalog to a plain Lua table array
```

```lua
DocEntry:hasReturnType() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry declares at least one return type
```

### `QualityReport`

Wraps documentation quality metrics for Lua access.

```lua
QualityReport:getBest( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns up to count entries with the highest quality scores

QualityReport:getGrade() -> string  -- Returns the letter grade for the overall score

QualityReport:getSummary() -> string  -- Returns a multi-line human-readable summary of quality by module

```

```lua
Schema:assert( data : table )  -- Validates data and throws a Lua error on failure with all error messages joined
Schema:check( data : table ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the data passes all schema rules
Schema:getFields() -> table  -- Returns a table of declared field names
```

### `ValidationReport`

Wraps a validation report for Lua access.

```lua
ValidationReport:getIncomplete() -> table  -- Returns the list of qualified names whose catalog entry is incomplete
ValidationReport:getMissing() -> table  -- Returns the list of qualified names present in the live API but missing from the catalog
```

## `lurek.patterns` {#patterns}

> Registers the `luna.patterns.*` software design patterns API.
>
> Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for reusable patterns:
> EventBus (prioritized pub-sub), ObjectPool (object reuse), CommandStack
> (undo/redo), ServiceLocator (DI container), Factory (named constructors),
> and SimpleState (finite state machine).

*Coverage: 103/103 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.patterns.newBlackboard( name : string? ) -> Blackboard  -- Creates a new Blackboard shared key-value store
lurek.patterns.newCommandStack( max_size : integer? ) -> CommandStack  -- Creates a new CommandStack instance
lurek.patterns.newDebounce( wait : number ) -> Debounce  -- Creates a trailing-edge debounce that fires after the input stream is idle for wait seconds
lurek.patterns.newEventBus( name : string? ) -> EventBus  -- Creates a new EventBus instance
lurek.patterns.newFactory() -> Factory  -- Creates a new Factory instance
lurek.patterns.newFunnel( window : number, max_entries : integer?, name : string? ) -> Funnel  -- Creates a time-windowed event aggregator. window=0 means flush on every push
lurek.patterns.newObjectPool() -> ObjectPool  -- Creates a new ObjectPool instance
lurek.patterns.newObserver( name : string? ) -> Observer  -- Creates a new reactive property Observer
lurek.patterns.newPriorityQueue( name : string? ) -> PriorityQueue  -- Creates a stable priority-ordered task queue
lurek.patterns.newRing( capacity : integer, name : string? ) -> Ring  -- Creates a fixed-capacity circular history buffer
lurek.patterns.newServiceLocator() -> ServiceLocator  -- Creates a new ServiceLocator instance
lurek.patterns.newSimpleState() -> SimpleState  -- Creates a new SimpleState finite state machine instance
lurek.patterns.newThrottle( interval : number ) -> Throttle  -- Creates a leading-edge rate limiter that fires at most once per interval seconds
```

### `Blackboard`

Lua wrapper for the Blackboard pattern.

```lua
Blackboard:clear( key : string )  -- Removes a fact from the blackboard
Blackboard:clearAll()  -- Clears all facts from the blackboard
Blackboard:get( key : string )  -- Gets a fact from the blackboard. Returns nil if not set
Blackboard:getRevision() -> integer  -- Returns the monotonic revision counter (incremented on every write)
Blackboard:has( key : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the key has a non-nil value
Blackboard:keys() -> table  -- Returns all set fact keys as a table
Blackboard:set( key : string, value : any )  -- Sets a fact on the blackboard. Accepts boolean, number, or string values
Blackboard:snapshot() -> table  -- Returns all facts as a flat key→value table
Blackboard:unwatch( id : integer )  -- Removes a watcher subscription by id
Blackboard:watch( key : string, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Subscribes to changes on a specific key (or "*" for all changes)
```

### `CommandStack`

Lua wrapper for the CommandStack pattern.

```lua
CommandStack:canRedo() -> boolean  -- Returns true if there is a command available to redo
CommandStack:canUndo() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the most recent command can be undone
CommandStack:clearAll()  -- Clears all command history, releasing Lua registry values
CommandStack:execute( name : string, exec_fn : function, undo_fn : function? )  -- Executes a named command and records it in undo/redo history
CommandStack:getCurrentName() -> string?  -- Returns the name of the most recently executed command, or nil
CommandStack:getHistorySize() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of recorded commands (undo + redo)
CommandStack:redo() -> boolean  -- Re-executes the next undone command. Returns true if successful
CommandStack:undo() -> boolean  -- Undoes the most recent command. Returns true if successful
```

### `Debounce`

Lua wrapper for the Debounce pattern.

```lua
Debounce:cancel()  -- Cancels the pending trigger without firing
Debounce:getFireCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of times this debounce has fired
Debounce:isPending() -> boolean  -- Returns true when a trigger is pending
Debounce:onFire( fn : function )  -- Sets the callback invoked when the debounce fires
Debounce:trigger()  -- Records an input event, resetting the idle timer
Debounce:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the idle timer by dt seconds; fires the callback if idle wait expired
```

### `EventBus`

Lua wrapper for the EventBus pattern.

```lua
EventBus:clear( event : string )  -- Removes all listeners for a specific event
EventBus:clearAll()  -- Removes all listeners on this EventBus
EventBus:emit( args : MultiValue )  -- Dispatches an event, calling all registered listeners in priority order
EventBus:getEvents() -> table  -- Returns all event names that have at least one listener
EventBus:getListenerCount( event : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of listeners registered for an event
EventBus:off( id : integer )  -- Removes a previously registered event listener by subscription ID
EventBus:on( event : string, callback : function, priority : integer? ) -> integer  -- Registers a listener callback for an event
```

### `Factory`

Lua wrapper for the Factory pattern.

```lua
Factory:alias( alias : string, canonical : string )  -- Registers an alias pointing to an existing canonical type name
Factory:clearAll()  -- Removes all registered type constructors and aliases
Factory:create( args : MultiValue )  -- Creates an instance of the named type by invoking its constructor
Factory:getTypes() -> table  -- Returns a table of all registered type names
Factory:has( type_name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the named type (or alias) is registered
Factory:register( type_name : string, ctor : function )  -- Registers a named type constructor function
Factory:remove( type_name : string )  -- Unregisters a type constructor (and any aliases pointing to it)
```

### `Funnel`

Lua wrapper for the Funnel (event aggregator) pattern.

```lua
Funnel:discard()  -- Discards all buffered entries without flushing
Funnel:flush()  -- Manually flushes all pending entries, invoking the onFlush callback
Funnel:getFlushCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of flushes performed
Funnel:onFlush( fn : function )  -- Sets a callback invoked when the funnel flushes. Receives a table of {tag, value} entries
Funnel:pendingCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of buffered entries not yet flushed
Funnel:push( tag : string, value : number? )  -- Adds an event to the funnel. Immediately flushes if max_entries reached or window is 0
Funnel:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the window timer by dt seconds; flushes when window expires
```

### `ObjectPool`

Lua wrapper for the ObjectPool pattern.

```lua
ObjectPool:acquire()  -- Acquires an available object from the pool; returns nil if empty
ObjectPool:add( value : any )  -- Inserts a pre-built object into the available pool
ObjectPool:clearAll()  -- Clears all objects from the pool, releasing Lua registry values
ObjectPool:getActiveCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of currently active (acquired) objects
ObjectPool:getAvailableCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of available (idle) objects in the pool
ObjectPool:getTotalCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of tracked objects (active + available)
ObjectPool:release( value : any )  -- Returns an object to the available pool
```

### `Observer`

Lua wrapper for the Observer pattern.

```lua
Observer:get( key : string )  -- Gets a property value, or nil if not set
Observer:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of active subscriptions
Observer:set( key : string, value : any )  -- Sets a property value and fires subscribed watchers
Observer:subscribe( key : string, callback : function, once : boolean? ) -> integer  -- Subscribes to changes on a property key (or "*" for all)
Observer:unsubscribe( id : integer )  -- Removes a subscription by id
```

### `PriorityQueue`

Lua wrapper for the PriorityQueue pattern.

```lua
PriorityQueue:clearAll()  -- Removes all items from the queue
PriorityQueue:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the queue has no items
PriorityQueue:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items in the queue
PriorityQueue:peek()  -- Returns the highest-priority item without removing it, or nil if empty
PriorityQueue:pop()  -- Removes and returns the highest-priority item, or nil if empty
PriorityQueue:push( priority : integer, value : any, label : string? ) -> integer  -- Inserts an item with a priority. Higher priorities are dequeued first
```

Lua wrapper for the Ring (circular buffer) pattern.

```lua

Ring:isFull() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the ring is at capacity
Ring:latest() -> table?  -- Returns the most recently pushed entry, or nil
Ring:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of entries currently in the ring
Ring:push( value : any, tag : string? ) -> integer  -- Pushes a value (number or string) with an optional tag. Overwrites oldest on overflow
Ring:sum() -> number  -- Returns the sum of all numeric values in the ring
Ring:toArray() -> table  -- Returns all entries (oldest first) as an array of {id, tag, value?, text?} tables
```

```lua
lurek.devtools.getProfileFrameCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of retained profile frames
lurek.devtools.getWatchInterval() -> number  -- Returns the file watch poll interval in seconds
lurek.devtools.getWatchedPaths() -> table  -- Returns an array of all watched paths
lurek.devtools.getWatches() -> table  -- Calls all registered watch getters and returns a table of {name, category, value} records
lurek.devtools.isConsoleOpen() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the console is considered open
lurek.devtools.isProfilingEnabled() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the profiler is enabled

lurek.devtools.openConsole() -> boolean  -- Opens the console window (updates the console flag; returns true)
lurek.devtools.profileFrame()  -- Seals the current frame of profiling data

lurek.devtools.removeWatch( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes a watch by the id returned from exposeWatch. Returns true if removed

lurek.devtools.scan() -> table  -- Polls all watched paths and returns paths whose mtime changed

lurek.devtools.setLogConsole( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables console log output
lurek.devtools.setLogFile( path : string )  -- Sets the log file path (empty string disables file output)

lurek.devtools.setProfilingEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables the profiler
lurek.devtools.setWatchInterval( interval : number )  -- Sets the file watch poll interval in seconds
lurek.devtools.snapshot() -> table  -- Takes a structured snapshot of all watches + frame stats + last profile frame
lurek.devtools.unwatch( path : string ) -> boolean  -- Removes a file path from the watch list
```

### `DrawLayer`

Lua-side z-ordered draw queue. Callbacks are sorted by z and called on `flush()`.

```lua
DrawLayer:clear() -> void  -- Removes all queued callbacks without calling them
DrawLayer:flush()  -- Sorts and calls all queued callbacks, then empties the queue
DrawLayer:getCount() -> number  -- Returns the number of queued callbacks
DrawLayer:queue( z : number, fn : function )  -- Queues a draw callback at the given z-order
DrawLayer:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name
DrawLayer:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is an instance of the given type name
```

### `NetworkHost`

```lua
NetworkHost:destroy() -> nil  -- Destroys the host, closing the underlying socket

NetworkHost:getConnectedPeerIds() -> table  -- Returns a table of connected peer IDs
NetworkHost:getPeerAddress( peer_id : integer ) -> string?  -- Returns the remote address of a peer, or nil if unavailable
NetworkHost:getPeerLimit() -> integer  -- Returns the maximum number of peer slots
NetworkHost:getPeerState( peer_id : integer ) -> string  -- Returns the connection state of a peer as a string
NetworkHost:getPeerStats( peer_id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns a statistics table for a peer
NetworkHost:getRoundTripTime( peer_id : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the round-trip time estimate for a peer in milliseconds
NetworkHost:isDestroyed() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the host has been destroyed

NetworkHost:resetPeer( peer_id : integer ) -> nil  -- Resets a peer connection immediately without notifying the remote side

```

*Coverage: 57/57 items documented (100%)*


### `Pipeline`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Pipeline`] DAG with scheduler and Lua callback registry.

```lua
Pipeline:addStep( step : PipelineStep ) -> Pipeline  -- Adds a step to the pipeline. Returns self for chaining
Pipeline:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels all pending and waiting steps
Pipeline:clear() -> nil  -- Clears all steps from the pipeline
Pipeline:getContext() -> table?  -- Returns the stored async context table, or nil
Pipeline:getErrorMode() -> string  -- Returns the current error mode as a string
Pipeline:getExecutionOrder() -> table?  -- Returns the topological execution order as an array of step names
Pipeline:getName() -> string  -- Returns the pipeline's name
Pipeline:getParallelGroups() -> table?  -- Returns parallel execution groups as a nested array of step name arrays
Pipeline:getResult() -> table?  -- Returns the current result table built from step states, or nil
Pipeline:getStep( name : string ) -> PipelineStep?  -- Returns the LuaStep wrapper for the named step, or nil
Pipeline:getStepCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of steps
Pipeline:getSteps() -> table  -- Returns a Lua array of all step wrappers in the pipeline
Pipeline:getStepsByTag( tag : string ) -> table  -- Returns a Lua array of all steps whose tag matches the given string
Pipeline:isComplete() -> boolean  -- Returns true if all steps have reached a terminal state
Pipeline:isRunning() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the pipeline is currently running asynchronously
Pipeline:removeStep( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a step from the pipeline by name
Pipeline:reset() -> nil  -- Resets all step states and clears the async context
Pipeline:run( context : table? ) -> table  -- Executes the pipeline synchronously in topological order
Pipeline:runAsync( context : table? ) -> nil  -- Starts an async pipeline run. Steps are executed one-per-frame via update(dt)
Pipeline:setErrorMode( mode : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the pipeline error mode: "abort" or "continue"
Pipeline:setName( name : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the pipeline's name
Pipeline:setOnComplete( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback to invoke when the pipeline completes
Pipeline:setOnStepError( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback to invoke each time a step fails
Pipeline:toTable() -> table  -- Serialises the pipeline definition to a Lua table (no callbacks)
Pipeline:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Pipeline:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
Pipeline:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the async pipeline by one tick. Returns true when all steps are done
Pipeline:validate() -> boolean  -- Validates the pipeline DAG. Returns (ok, error_array)
```

### `Step`

Lua-side wrapper around a single [`PipelineStep`], plus Lua callback registry keys.

```lua
Step:dependsOn( dep : string ) -> PipelineStep  -- Adds a dependency on another step by name or PipelineStep. Returns self for chaining
Step:getAttempt() -> integer  -- Returns the number of execution attempts so far
Step:getData( key : string ) -> string?  -- Retrieves a metadata value by key, returning nil if not found
Step:getDelay() -> number  -- Returns the configured delay in seconds
Step:getDependencies() -> table  -- Returns the list of dependency step names
Step:getDependencyCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of declared dependencies
Step:getDuration() -> number  -- Returns total seconds spent executing this step
Step:getError() -> string?  -- Returns the error message from the last failed attempt, or nil
Step:getName() -> string  -- Returns the unique name of this step
Step:getRetryCount() -> integer  -- Returns the configured retry count
Step:getStatus() -> string  -- Returns the current execution status as a string
Step:getTag() -> string?  -- Returns the tag on this step, or nil if unset
Step:getTimeout() -> number  -- Returns the timeout stored in metadata, or 0.0 if unset
Step:isOptional() -> boolean  -- Returns whether this step is marked as optional
Step:setCallback( fn : function ) -> nil  -- Stores a Lua function as the execute callback for this step
Step:setCondition( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Stores a Lua function (or nil) as the run-condition for this step
Step:setData( key : string, value : string ) -> nil  -- Stores an arbitrary string value under the given key in step metadata
Step:setDelay( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the delay in seconds to wait after dependencies finish
Step:setOnError( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Stores a Lua function (or nil) to call if this step fails
Step:setOptional( optional : boolean ) -> nil  -- Marks whether this step is optional (downstream steps continue on failure)
Step:setRetryCount( count : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the maximum number of retry attempts on failure
Step:setRetryDelay( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the delay in seconds between retry attempts
Step:setTag( tag : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the tag on this step for grouping and filtering
Step:setTimeout( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Stores a timeout in seconds in the step's metadata
Step:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name "PipelineStep"
Step:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the given name matches "PipelineStep" or a parent type
```

## `lurek.tween` {#tween}

> `luna.tween` — thin Lua registration wrapper for the property tween system.
>
> # Purpose

*Coverage: 19/19 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.tween.cancelAll() -> nil  -- Cancels all active tweens, sequences, and parallels immediately
lurek.tween.delay( seconds : number, fn : function ) -> TweenSequence  -- Creates a no-op tween that waits `seconds`, then optionally calls `callback`
lurek.tween.getActiveCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of currently active tween objects (tweens + seqs + pars)
lurek.tween.getEasingNames() -> table  -- Returns a list of all available easing names (built-in + custom)
lurek.tween.parallel() -> TweenParallel  -- Creates an empty TweenParallel. Add entries with :tween() or :add(tween),
lurek.tween.registerEasing( name : string, fn : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a custom easing function under `name`. `fn(t)` receives 0..1, returns 0..1
lurek.tween.sequence() -> TweenSequence  -- Creates an empty TweenSequence. Add steps with :tween(), :delay(), :callback(),
lurek.tween.tween( duration : number, target : table, fields : table, easing : string ) -> Tween  -- Creates a new property tween and registers it for automatic updating
lurek.tween.update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances all active tweens, sequences, and parallels by `dt` seconds
```

### `Tween`

```lua
Tween:getProgress() -> number  -- Returns raw 0..1 playback progress (not eased, not accounting for yoyo)
Tween:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the tween is still running (not completed or cancelled)
Tween:pause() -> nil  -- Pauses this tween; time stops advancing but the tween is not cancelled
Tween:resume() -> nil  -- Resumes a paused tween
Tween:setRepeat( n : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the number of extra play cycles after the first (0 = play once, -1 = infinite)
Tween:setYoyo( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables yoyo (ping-pong) on each repeat cycle
```

### `TweenParallel`

```lua
TweenParallel:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels the parallel group immediately
TweenParallel:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the parallel is running and not yet complete
```

### `TweenSequence`

```lua
TweenSequence:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels the sequence and stops all pending steps
TweenSequence:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the sequence has been started and has not yet completed
```
